claim_summary: Education is primarily the responsibility of the 16 Länder (federal states) under the German Basic Law (Articles 30 and 70). Each Land has its own ministry of education and enacts its own school legislation. The Standing Conference of the Ministers of Education and Cultural Affairs (KMK) coordinates common standards and agreements among the Länder; KMK resolutions are recommendations that become binding only when enacted by individual Land parliaments. School types, curricula, and examination rules vary by Land. There is no federal ministry with legislative authority over schools.
source_section_or_page: KMK/Eurydice dossier: Educational legislation and administration of the education system are primarily the responsibility of the Länder; KMK resolutions have the status of recommendations until enacted as binding legislation by the parliaments in the Länder
notes: Primary languages cleared to empty array and grade_structure cleared to empty object due to lack of direct federal source. Curriculum and framework downgraded to needs_research due to schema limitations.

claim_summary: Three credits required for the Foundation program (4 for the default endorsement path). Must include Algebra I and Geometry. Additional advanced mathematics credits per 19 TAC 74.12(b)(2); the default endorsement path adds one additional advanced mathematics course per 19 TAC 74.13(e)(2).

claim_summary: Three credits required. One credit U.S. History. One-half credit U.S. Government. One-half credit Economics or Personal Financial Literacy. One credit World History or World Geography per 19 TAC 74.12(b)(4).

claim_summary: Two credits required. May be satisfied by two levels in the same language (including ASL), or two credits in computer programming languages per 19 TAC 74.12(b)(5). Substitutions are permitted for students with disabilities per district committee determination.

claim_summary: Five elective credits required for the Foundation plan. Additional endorsement courses may also count toward elective requirements. Electives must be selected from state-approved courses per 19 TAC 74.12(c).

claim_summary: Four additional credits required for the default endorsement path (default for students entering 9th grade since 2014-15). Includes one additional mathematics credit, one additional science credit, and two endorsement-specific elective credits. Students may opt out of an endorsement after sophomore year with parent permission per 19 TAC 74.11(e). Endorsement areas: STEM, Business and Industry, Public Services, Arts and Humanities, Multidisciplinary Studies.
source_authority: Texas State Board of Education / Texas Education Agency (19 TAC)

claim_summary: Specific courses: ["English I","English II","Algebra I","Biology","U.S. History"]
source_authority: Texas State Board of Education / Texas Education Agency (19 TAC)

claim_summary: Students must pass five STAAR End-of-Course (EOC) assessments as specified in 19 TAC Chapter 101 (Assessment). Students who do not pass up to two exams may still graduate through an Individual Graduation Committee (IGC) per TEC 28.025(c). Beginning 2022-23, students must complete a college and career readiness assessment in English and math (or TSI alternative).
source_authority: Texas State Board of Education / Texas Education Agency (19 TAC)
Texas State Board of Education / Texas Education Agency (19 TAC)

claim_summary: Subject category: Speech and Communication Proficiency
source_authority: Texas State Board of Education / Texas Education Agency (19 TAC)
Texas State Board of Education / Texas Education Agency (19 TAC)

claim_summary: Demonstrated proficiency in delivering clear verbal messages, choosing effective nonverbal behaviors, listening for desired results, applying critical-thinking and problem-solving processes, and identifying, analyzing, developing, and evaluating communication skills. Must be demonstrated in Grade 8 or higher as determined by the district per 19 TAC 74.11(a)(3). A specific course is not required; proficiency may be demonstrated through district-approved methods.
source_authority: Texas State Board of Education / Texas Education Agency (19 TAC)
Texas State Board of Education / Texas Education Agency (19 TAC)

claim_summary: Students must pass five STAAR End-of-Course (EOC) assessments: English I, English II, Algebra I, Biology, and U.S. History. Additional non-course requirements include speech/communication proficiency and FAFSA/TASFA completion.
